mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to corporate earnings, mechanically increasing equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 8.5% and monthly payrolls increase by more than 100,000, labor market normalization confirms economic recovery, triggering risk-on positioning and positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 8.5% while PAYEMS rises > 100k in 3 months
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite meeting the labor market thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand expansion, which lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero correlation with 20-day forward SPX returns across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain resilience, which supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative when INDPRO rises > 1.5% quarter-over-quarter during high inflation periods (CPI > 4%).

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP directly increases aggregate corporate earnings, which mechanically lifts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days following each data release.
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 revisions and 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.05 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 0.5% and CPI surpasses 3.0%, the Federal Reserve is forced to tighten policy aggressively to combat inflation, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 0.5% while CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in > 60% of instances where both thresholds are breached.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate combined with declining monthly payrolls signals deteriorating labor demand, which reduces consumer spending power and corporate cash flows, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint deterioration in UNRATE and PAYEMS fails to predict negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 40% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts drop below 500,000 units while real GDP growth remains positive, the housing sector decouples from the broader economy, signaling a localized demand shock that triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances despite the housing collapse.
